danger_signals.visual_form: Flashing highlight on defensive player closest to ball
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat zone; player must respond to prevent scoring

danger_signals.visual_form: Ball path from batter toward field
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger to fielders; collision/miss risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Player selection highlight; pitcher control during wind-up
safety_signals.meaning: Player has agency and direction over action

safety_signals.visual_form: Runner position on bases (implied visual state)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe staging area; strategic advancement opportunity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing batting team visually distinct on field
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pitcher's mound perspective; home plate frame of reference

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fielders distribute across outfield and infield zones
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Standard baseball diamond layout with positional staging

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Directional input options for pitch selection (four cardinal directions for type and location)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cycles through pitch types; aim toward low/high/inside/outside targets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Batter positioned in box; directional aim controls for swing contact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player timing swing and direction matching pitch trajectory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Active fielder flashes/highlights to indicate control assignment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Closest defensive player automatically selected; player uses four directions to throw to bases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Team roster and statistics interface displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player rearranges lineup, selects pitcher from roster, manages substitutions based on individual player stats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field perspective from pitcher's mound (broadcast-style camera angle)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous play cycle: pitch → hit/miss → field → throw → base advance

environmental_storytelling.example: Pitcher's mound view mirrors MLB broadcast standard, immediately communicates 'professional baseball' context through familiar camera angle
Flashing fielder indicator shows who 'owns' the ball without dialogue; visual feedback replaces text explanation
Each team member has own stats affecting performance; managerial interface displays individual capability variance, signaling strategic depth
Four-cardinal-direction control scheme maps to pitch location (low/high/inside/outside), creating intuitive spatial language for action intention

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Player selects pitch type via directional input; wind-up animation prepares throw
Accepts directional commands for aim adjustment before release
Stance in box; responds to incoming pitch with directional swing timing
Runs bases automatically if hit; visual trajectory determines success/failure
Flashes/highlights to show current control; intercepts ball and throws to designated base
Holds position; awaits ball or throw assignment
Makes pitch/swing/field decisions autonomously; described as 'unrealistic' and 'low difficulty' in reviews, suggesting predictable patterns

notes: HardBall III (Genesis 1992) source data limited to Wikipedia entry describing original 1985 HardBall! series. No specific visual design documentation for Genesis iteration available. Manifestation patterns inferred from gameplay mechanics described: pitcher's mound perspective, flashing fielder indicator, four-directional control system, and managerial statistical overlay. Game communicates game-state primarily through control paradigm shifts (pitcher → batter → fielder phase transitions) and visual highlight feedback (flashing to indicate active entity). No faction colors/symbols documented; baseball inherently opposes pitcher vs. batter and defense vs. offense through positional field arrangement. World-state manifestation relies on UI integration (managerial overlay) and perspective anchoring (broadcast-style camera). Environmental storytelling achieved through perspective familiarity and behavioral UI rather than narrative diegesis.
